first of all, you can never get hold of anyone on the phone that can speak english so you go into the store and they are so rude at every store we’ve been to, but this one was the worst in oak ridge. there was absolutely no one in there except us and three workers it was like none of them wanted to help us. we canceled all of our services except for internet, and that will be coming soon. the guy could not work with us at all about coming down on the internet prices. you give a new customer a price of $70 a month and ours is 143 and no we did not want the one we have. he said we had to have it. we will be canceling that very soon.

1. i feel like i was lied to about bundles on store. when i checked my app it said my monthly bills were going to be $165 when i was told it’d be $75. over the phone they were able to get my bills lowered but also told me that what they had told me in store wasn’t true and it was not a bundle. after the phone call i got 29 text messages in a 4 minute window wanting me to approve a $280 order that i did not want or order so i never approved it. i still am not sure exactly wha tht services are, what i will owe, and feel like my entire experience has been extremely difficult when all i wanted was simple wifi. i will never recommend xfinity to anyone and once i don’t have to have it as my service will drop it immediately

i went yesterday since i had gone before to ask if i can switch to you from verizon bringing my 5 lines with me to xfinity now and i was told that i was good to go with no down payment but since i still had to stay with verizon for 2 months i had to wait but i still went ahead and got the internet and one line which they had told me it was going to be free but at the end i had to pay $20 because it was an ipad which ok i let that go and now when i went yesterday to transfer my 5 i also asked about getting new phones for at least 3 lines and when i was there i was given a different amount of credit for 2 of them and asked for a breakdown and was told that i had to wait for the trade ins and when i got home and checked my bank account i saw a deduction of $556 which to me was ridiculous so i went back today to get a breakdown and the same guy said that i forgot my receipt which was a lie since i asked for 1 yesterday and at the end it was a $300 difference and was told to call corporate and see if i can get credited the difference and now i’m thinking of returning everything and going to another provider which it’s still in the window to return everything.

i am incredibly grateful to xfinity for the opportunities, support, and growth i’ve experienced throughout my journey with the company. xfinity has provided an environment where i have been able to develop my leadership skills, build meaningful relationships with both customers and team members, and continuously challenge myself to achieve higher goals. the company’s commitment to innovation, customer service, and employee development inspires me every day. being part of a team that works hard to connect people and make a positive impact in our communities is something i take great pride in. i appreciate the trust, guidance, and encouragement i’ve received, and i am proud to represent a company that values excellence, teamwork, and dedication. xfinity has helped me grow both professionally and personally, and i look forward to continuing to contribute to its success while helping customers find the best solutions for their needs.
